title: Error: ‘vlProcess’ not declared in coroutine
body:
### Issue Description
The error only occurs under certain conditions, and unfortunately, I cannot share the code due to confidentiality. This makes it difficult to reproduce. However, I want to know if anyone has encountered this error before and knows how to resolve it.

Additionally, I noticed that this coroutine does not include vlProcess as an argument, whereas other coroutines do. I am unsure why this discrepancy exists.

Interestingly, the issue seems to depend on the size of the testbench. For example, my testbench currently runs 27 tests. If I comment out **any** of the tests, the issue no longer occurs. This suggests the problem might be triggered when the testbench reaches a certain size or complexity.

### Command Line Flags
```bash
verilator --top-module my_tb --cc --exe --build --main --Mdir /my_mdir --threads 1 --assert --timing --trace-fst -Wall -Wno-PINCONNECTEMPTY -Wno-UNUSEDSIGNAL -Wno-UNUSEDPARAM -Wno-DECLFILENAME -Wno-GENUNNAMED -Wno-MODDUP -Wno-ALWCOMBORDER -Wno-WIDTHEXPAND -Wno-UNOPTFLAT -Wno-BLKANDNBLK -Wno-MULTIDRIVEN -Wno-IMPURE -Wno-WIDTHCONCAT
```

### Verilator Version
Verilator 5.031 devel rev v5.030-79-g58dae8f93 (latest)

### Error Output
```
Vmy_tb_my_tb__DepSet_hf2d76328__0.cpp:1373:77: error: ‘vlProcess’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘VlProcess’?
 1373 |                                                                             vlProcess,
      |                                                                             ^~~~~~~~~
      |                                                                             VlProcess
Vmy_tb_my_tb__DepSet_hf2d76328__0.cpp:1383:57: error: ‘vlProcess’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘VlProcess’?
 1383 |                                                         vlProcess,
      |                                                         ^~~~~~~~~
      |                                                         VlProcess
Vmy_tb_my_tb__DepSet_hf2d76328__0.cpp:1703:5: error: ‘vlProcess’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘VlProcess’?
 1703 |     vlProcess->state(VlProcess::FINISHED);
      |     ^~~~~~~~~
      |     VlProcess
Vmy_tb_my_tb__DepSet_hf2d76328__0.cpp:1705:1: warning: control reaches end of non-void function [-Wreturn-type]
 1705 | }
      | ^
make[1]: *** [/usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk:269: Vmy_tb_my_tb__DepSet_hf2d76328__0.o] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory 'my_dir'
%Error: make -C my_dir -f Vmy_tb.mk -j 1 exited with 2
```

### Additional Context
The coroutine in question:
```cpp
VlCoroutine Vmy_tb_my_tb___eval_initial__TOP__my_tb__Vtiming__1__1(Vmy_tb_my_tb* vlSelf) { 
    ...
    while (VL_LTS_III(32, 0U, __Vtask_advance_clock__642__unnamedblk1_1__DOT____Vrepeat0)) {
        co_await vlSymsp->TOP.__VtrigSched_h87bff3fb__0.trigger(
            0U, 
            vlProcess, 
            "@(posedge my_tb.clk)", 
            "my_dir/my_tb.sv", 
            131
        );
        vlSymsp->TOP.__Vm_traceActivity[0x96U] = 1U;
        __Vtask_advance_clock__642__unnamedblk1_1__DOT____Vrepeat0 = 
            (__Vtask_advance_clock__642__unnamedblk1_1__DOT____Vrepeat0 - (IData)(1U));
    }
}
```

I don’t know why this coroutine does not include vlProcess as an argument, while I see that many other coroutines do. Any insight into this would also be appreciated.

If you’ve encountered this error or have suggestions, I’d appreciate any guidance on how to resolve it.
